anchor: In Yoga psychology, citta refers to the total cognitive field in which thoughts, impressions, and perceptions arise.
positive: In cognitive neuroscience, large-scale brain networks provide the dynamic neural workspace within which thoughts and perceptions emerge.

anchor: Citta-vṛtti-nirodha describes the systematic regulation and stilling of fluctuations within the cognitive field.
positive: Executive inhibitory control mechanisms suppress ongoing neural activity to stabilize goal-directed processing.

anchor: Vṛtti refers to dynamic mental modifications that continuously reshape conscious experience.
positive: Spontaneous neural activity reflects ongoing intrinsic fluctuations that shape cognitive experience.

anchor: Nirodha represents the deliberate suppression and stabilization of mental processes through disciplined regulation.
positive: Response inhibition in the prefrontal cortex suppresses prepotent or competing neural responses.

anchor: Asmita describes the misidentification of pure awareness with cognitive structures and self-models.
positive: Self-referential processing in the default mode network constructs and maintains self-representations.

anchor: Avidya refers to a fundamental cognitive misapprehension that confuses transient mental states with enduring identity.
positive: Predictive coding errors occur when internal generative models misrepresent external or internal signals.

anchor: Raga represents attachment formed through repeated pleasurable cognitive associations.
positive: Reward-based reinforcement learning strengthens neural pathways associated with positive outcomes.

anchor: Dvesha represents aversion formed through repeated painful cognitive associations.
positive: Amygdala-mediated threat processing encodes and reinforces aversive experiences.

anchor: Samskara refers to latent cognitive impressions that condition future mental patterns and behaviors.
positive: Habit memory traces in basal ganglia circuits influence future behavioral responses.

anchor: Smriti describes the reactivation of stored cognitive impressions without distortion.
positive: Episodic memory consolidation in the hippocampus stabilizes stored experiential representations.

anchor: Dharana involves the intentional fixation of attention upon a single cognitive object.
positive: Sustained attention network activation maintains neural focus on a specific stimulus.

anchor: Dhyana represents the uninterrupted flow of attention toward a chosen object without distraction.
positive: Continuous executive network engagement supports sustained task-focused cognition.

anchor: Samadhi describes a state of deep absorption in which cognitive fluctuations are minimized.
positive: Large-scale neural synchronization occurs during highly focused cognitive states.

anchor: Pratyahara represents the withdrawal of sensory engagement from external stimuli.
positive: Top-down sensory gating mechanisms suppress irrelevant sensory inputs in cortical circuits.

anchor: Ekagrata refers to the stabilization of cognition into a one-pointed attentional state.
positive: Reduced attentional switching cost reflects increased executive stability.

anchor: Vairagya denotes the reduction of emotional attachment to cognitive content.
positive: Extinction learning reduces conditioned emotional responses in reward circuitry.

anchor: Kleshas are afflictive cognitive distortions that bias perception and behavior.
positive: Maladaptive emotional regulation circuitry contributes to biased cognitive processing.

anchor: Buddhi represents the discriminative faculty that differentiates awareness from mental content.
positive: Dorsolateral prefrontal cortex supports cognitive discrimination and executive selection.

anchor: Purusha is described as witnessing consciousness distinct from cognitive fluctuations.
positive: Meta-awareness networks monitor cognitive processes without directly generating them.

anchor: Prajna refers to higher-order insight emerging from stabilized cognition.
positive: Metacognitive accuracy reflects higher-order monitoring of cognitive states.

anchor: Nirodha-parinama describes progressive transformation toward mental stillness.
positive: Neural plasticity under sustained cognitive training reshapes network dynamics.

anchor: Samadhi-parinama refers to the shift from distributed cognition to one-pointed absorption.
positive: Network reconfiguration toward task-focused stability reduces distributed processing.

anchor: Ekagrata-parinama describes sequential stabilization of attentional states.
positive: Attentional stabilization dynamics reflect progressive executive refinement.

anchor: The dissolution of asmita reduces identification with cognitive content.
positive: Reduced medial prefrontal activity correlates with decreased self-referential processing.

anchor: Samyama integrates concentration, meditation, and absorption into unified cognitive control.
positive: Synchronized frontoparietal network coupling integrates attention and executive control.

anchor: Mental coloring in Yoga describes affective bias influencing perception.
positive: Affective bias in neural processing alters perception and decision-making.

anchor: The purification of citta reduces cognitive noise and distortion.
positive: Increased signal-to-noise ratio in cortical processing enhances cognitive clarity.

anchor: Rajas represents agitation and excessive cognitive activation.
positive: Heightened sympathetic arousal increases neural excitability.

anchor: Tamas represents inertia and diminished cognitive responsiveness.
positive: Reduced cortical activation correlates with decreased responsiveness.

anchor: Sattva represents clarity and balanced cognitive functioning.
positive: Optimized network coherence supports efficient cognitive processing.

anchor: Withdrawal of the senses reduces external cognitive interference.
positive: Cortical sensory inhibition reduces external stimulus processing.

anchor: Objectless concentration describes awareness without specific cognitive content.
positive: Minimal default mode network activation reflects reduced self-referential processing.

anchor: The stabilization of mind-field reduces internal entropy.
positive: Reduced neural entropy reflects increased network stability.

anchor: Training of attention strengthens sustained cognitive regulation.
positive: Executive attention strengthening enhances task control.

anchor: Transcendence of mental objects reduces stimulus-driven cognition.
positive: Decoupling of stimulus-driven processing reduces reactive neural activity.

anchor: Separation of seer and seen differentiates awareness from cognitive content.
positive: Meta-cognitive decoupling separates self-models from sensory input.

anchor: Breath regulation modulates internal physiological states.
positive: Autonomic nervous system modulation alters physiological arousal.

anchor: Steady posture supports mental stability.
positive: Reduced motor cortex excitability correlates with physical stillness.

anchor: Latent impressions resurfacing disrupt cognitive stability.
positive: Reactivation of stored memory traces influences ongoing neural processing.

anchor: Progressive meditative absorption reorganizes cognition.
positive: Long-term meditation induces large-scale neural network reorganization.

anchor: Reduction of afflictive patterns improves perceptual accuracy.
positive: Improved emotional regulation enhances cognitive accuracy.

anchor: Discriminative insight reduces cognitive confusion.
positive: Enhanced executive monitoring reduces decision-making errors.

anchor: One-pointed attention reduces cognitive fragmentation.
positive: Focused neural activation reduces distributed network noise.

anchor: Sustained contemplative practice reshapes cognitive habits.
positive: Repeated training induces experience-dependent neuroplasticity.

anchor: Cognitive stillness reduces reactive mental activity.
positive: Suppression of spontaneous neural activity reduces reactivity.

anchor: Higher awareness monitors cognitive fluctuations.
positive: Higher-order monitoring processes track internal neural states.

anchor: Mental fluctuation identification leads to disidentification.
positive: Awareness of self-referential activation reduces default mode engagement.

anchor: Non-attachment reduces compulsive cognitive reinforcement.
positive: Reduced dopaminergic reinforcement lowers habitual responding.
